title: Your first loop
instruction:
A loop repeats an action. Write the action once, tell it how many times.

Run this to print five shelf numbers without writing five Println lines.
successMessage: 5 lines from 3 lines of loop code. That's the power of repetition.

title: Loop over a slice with range
instruction:
Use `for i, book := range books` to loop over every item in a slice. `i` is the index, `book` is the value.

Add "The Hobbit" and "Neuromancer" to the `books` slice, then print each one.

codeChecks.message: Add "The Hobbit" to the books slice.
Use for i, book := range books to loop.
Replace the TODO comment with your books.

successMessage: range gives you both the position and the value — no manual indexing needed.

title: Skip the index with _
instruction:
Sometimes you only want the value, not the index. Use `_` to ignore it.

Print just the book titles (no index numbers).

codeChecks.message: Use for _, book := range books to skip the index.
Print only the book variable, not the index.

successMessage: _ tells Go "ignore this." Cleaner output, clearer intent.

title: Loop over a map
instruction:
Maps and range work together. `for isbn, title := range catalog` gives you every key-value pair.

Print each ISBN and title from the catalog.
successMessage: range works on maps too. One loop, all entries.

title: Break out early
instruction:
Use `break` to exit a loop immediately. When you find what you need, stop searching.

This loop processes 5 "requests." After the loop, print "All requests processed" to confirm you exited cleanly.
hint: Add fmt.Println("All requests processed") after the closing brace of the for loop.

codeChecks.message: Add fmt.Println("All requests processed") after the loop.
Replace the TODO comment with your Println line.

successMessage: break jumps out. Code after the loop keeps running normally.

title: Running total 🤯
instruction:
A loop can accumulate a result. This is called a running total.

Make this loop sum 1 through 100. The result should be 5050 — the famous Gauss total.

codeChecks.message: Replace the TODO comments with your code.

successMessage: 5050. A loop that adds. Running totals are the foundation of stats, counters, and aggregations.

title: Continue — skip the rest of this iteration
instruction:
`continue` skips the current iteration and moves to the next one. Use it to filter.

Print only the book titles longer than 4 characters. Skip shorter ones with `continue`.

codeChecks.message: Use continue to skip books with short titles.
Use len(book) to check the title length.
Replace the TODO comment with your continue condition.

successMessage: continue says "not this one, move on." Perfect for filtering inside a loop.

title: Nested loops — loop inside a loop
instruction:
A loop inside another loop creates combinations. For each shelf, print 3 books.

The outer loop runs 3 times, the inner loop runs 3 times each. That's 9 total iterations.

Make the inner loop print each book number on a shelf.

codeChecks.message: Create an inner loop with for book := 1; book <= 3; book++
Replace the TODO comment with your inner loop.

successMessage: Nested loops: for each X, do every Y. Powerful for grids, combinations, and multi-level data.
